title: Life Running Digital Engine
html:
This is comprehensively composed of all the sub-systems for how I run most aspects of my life where a rule-engine system is employable. The idea is to treat the recurring decisions of daily life less like fresh judgment calls and more like rules that have already been decided once and can simply run.

This system’s overall implementation strongly prefers using apps. The reason is to leverage app notifications, along with the First-screen Principle, to alleviate the mental tax of decision making and the constant anxiety of “doing the right thing”. When the right cue surfaces at the right moment, you don’t have to remember or deliberate — you just act.

Above all of the sub-systems is the mindset that governs their execution. The current model to adhere to is States of Mind.

Driver Todo System

You take the driver seat.

App: Things

Every task and project is an opportunity to pick up. They have a positive desirable outcome to pursue. Every progress pushes your life forward.

The performance is not preallocated, not time-scheduled. The mentality when executing the task backlog while you are in the driver seat is one of high urgency: “could I do this with the minimal time?”, or “could I do the best job for this in 30 minutes?”. The faster I accomplish the outcome, the better.

This excludes routine, scheduled tasks. This goal-todo system contains opportunities, not obligations (there’s a YouTube video from Ali Abdaal regarding this).

Related: Healthy todo backlog

Accountability Todo System

Non-negotiable, executed with no wasted mental cycle — that is the beauty of the system. What is settled in advance no longer needs to be argued with in the moment.

Apps: Calendar + Reminders + Streaks

The system manages the obligations and routines that I just mindlessly do. Most of them are time-scheduled or have a very clear do-able context.

The “when to do it” rule:

  • When it is due — evening for date-scheduled, when it appears for datetime-scheduled.
  • In context (location, equipment). There’s only a single context now:
    • Groceries: when I’m at a grocery store.

Reflection Cadence

Reflection cadence is the system dynamic of self-stabilizing evolution. Reflection sessions are performed routinely, on recurrent schedules, so the system keeps correcting itself rather than quietly drifting.

There are three cycles, daily, weekly and monthly. They are scheduled with noted details within the Reminders app:

  • The Day
  • Weekly Reflection
  • Monthly Reflection

(One of the more systematic ideas I keep returning to in a friend's garden of thoughts.)

  • Programmable Self
